title: bug: Correct Docker compat REST API image delete endpoint
body:
The docker `-XDELETE image/$name?force=true` endpoint only remove containers using an image if they are in a stopped state. In Podman, when forcefully removing images we also delete running containers.

This patch changes the Docker image force delete to be behaviour like the Docker API while maintaining commands like `podman rmi -f $imagename`

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25871

<!--

-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Changes the -XDELETE /images/{name:.*} Docker compat API endpoint to no longer force delete running containers using the image being requested to forcefully delete.
```

comments.body: Code LGTM, just needs a test
agree, code LGTM so far.  Needs a test, can you also confirm there is a test to protect the podman behavior which is different? If there is not one, write one please.

> agree, code LGTM so far. Needs a test, can you also confirm there is a test to protect the podman behavior which is different? If there is not one, write one please.

I have added a test to protect this variant between Docker and Podman

`podman rmi -f $image` force deleting the image and force deleting containers using the image is protected by [this test](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/a3e132055d8f9fd52f1bb79a57b1e3ffc9929e6d/test/system/010-images.bats#L283-L319) IMO, please let me know what you think.

Some unhappy tests.
Other than that, and @Honny1 's last comment, LGTM.

> Some unhappy tests. Other than that, and @Honny1 's last comment, LGTM.

Thanks for the patience on this one folks, first time working with out API tests.
